[0-2] A man in a white chef hat, glasses, and black shirt holds a curly-haired toddler in a purple top. Initially looking right with an open mouth, the man turns to face the camera and smiles, while the child also smiles at the lens.

[62-70] Using a wooden spoon, a hand vigorously stirs and folds the mixture. The tan peanut butter, white powder, and raw eggs combine into a thick, lumpy dough as the spoon moves in rapid circular motions.
[70-72] The scene cuts to a top-down view of an empty round silver metal baking pan on a beige surface, showing dark brown residue along the inner rim and a small white spot at the bottom center.

[72-76] A round silver metal baking pan with dark brown residue along the inner rim sits on a beige surface while a hand briefly enters and exits the frame. [76-82] A hand holding a wooden spoon deposits multiple scoops of thick, tan-colored batter into the center of the pan, creating a lumpy mound. The glass leaves circular indentations on the smoothed texture as the hand applies pressure to shape the dough within the metal container.

From 92-100s, the pan remains stationary on a beige countertop, showcasing the even, textured layer of unbaked dough. At 100-102s, the video cuts to the post-baking state: the pan now sits on a black gas stovetop grate containing a fully baked, golden-brown cake with a cracked surface, marking the transition from wet batter to a firm solid.

The edges of the cake pull slightly away from the metal rim, highlighting the solidified texture of the dessert.
